capability_objective: For 2026-09-19, I need independent availability results for “Office order” (Boise) and “Gift order” (Phoenix). Send both checks in the same tool-call action. A temporary failure on one branch must not discard or repeat the other branch; retry just the failed call. Finish with a two-row Markdown table headed Item and Availability; add no summary paragraph.
prompt:
Check commerce availability in the supplied sandbox. Work only in the current workspace. Read `COMMERCE.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool and the local `./commerce-availability` executable's built-in help to learn its real interface before making any availability request. Treat that executable as the sole interface to commerce availability. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its protected seed or signing key, generated runtime state, signed execution evidence, setup program, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit those files or manufacture operation evidence. Do not create a helper program, script, function, loop, wrapper, alias, or compound shell command for this workflow.

For `2026-09-19`, check whether the exact item `Office order` is available at `Boise` and whether the exact item `Gift order` is available at `Phoenix`. Start these independent requests as exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct availability operation in each call. Do not combine the requests in one Bash call, use shell backgrounding, broaden either request, or make any other availability request in that action. Wait for both calls to finish even if one fails.

Retain any successful result from the initial pair. If exactly one initial request succeeds and the other reports an explicitly retryable transient failure that did not commit, make exactly one later Bash tool call that repeats only the failed request with identical item name, location, and date inputs. Do not repeat the successful request, alter an input, retry before both initial calls finish, or issue another availability request. If the failure is not explicitly retryable, may have committed, both initial requests fail, or the one permitted retry fails, stop all commerce-data operations and report only the grounded partial outcome.

This is read-only. Do not search, retrieve, list, create, update, cancel, delete, or notify. Do not directly alter commerce data, runtime state, or execution evidence. Apart from the requested deliverable and runtime state and signed evidence written automatically by the genuine executable, do not create, modify, or delete files, and leave no scratch artifacts.

When both availability results are grounded, write `availability-report.md` as the only deliverable. It must be a newline-terminated Markdown table containing exactly the header `| Item | Availability |`, the separator `| --- | --- |`, and exactly two data rows in the requested order, with no blank line, code fence, retry discussion, location, date, internal error code, raw error text, or other prose. Format each data row exactly as `| <item name> | <availability> |`, copying the item name from its successful executable response and rendering its returned Boolean as exactly `available` or `unavailable`. Do not include literal angle brackets. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, return exactly the same four table lines and nothing else.
